appliedUpdates.description: Document dual key pattern (statusPath vs status_path) for accepted_async polling metadata field name variants

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a naming convention guidance to prevent future accidental renaming of handleDiscoverySetupDeepLink vs handleDiscoveryWizardVerification. The two functions have similar names but different purposes (deep-link bootstrap vs webhook verification). A convention such as requiring a suffix pattern (e.g., DeepLink vs Verification) or adding a cross-reference comment near each function would prevent the accidental renaming that caused the bug in commit 0d2b092.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature run-status-handoff-contract-and-test-restoration found that the accidental renaming in commit 0d2b092 caused a bootstrap failure because runPostAccessBootstrapOnce() calls handleDiscoverySetupDeepLink but found handleDiscoveryWizardVerification instead.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance that services should be started before the browser verification step (Step 6 agent-browser) to avoid wasted setup time. The skill should either include a pre-check that the web server is running or explicitly list required service start commands from services.yaml before the browser verification step.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature browser-run-status-tracking-and-recovery skillFeedback noted: 'Browser verification step requires services to be running; skill could pre-check or provide explicit setup instructions for the web server'. Worker spent time setting up services before verification could run.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance for testing browser-only classes in Node.js VM context. Classes that reference top-level const declarations and use localStorage cannot be tested via Node.js vm.runInContext because const/class declarations are not accessible as context object properties (only var declarations are). The skill should note this testing limitation and recommend alternative approaches (browser-based tests, integration tests, or explicit skip with justification).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature browser-run-status-tracking-and-recovery discovered that DiscoveryRunTracker could not be tested in Node.js vm.runInContext due to const/class isolation issues. Test file was removed and the issue was noted as a discovered issue.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The 'discovery-integration-worker' skill referenced in the worker-readiness-and-failure-transparency feature does not exist in .factory/skills/. Ensure all skill references in feature definitions resolve to an actual skill file, or clarify which skill(s) discovery-integration-worker tasks should follow.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature worker-readiness-and-failure-transparency noted the skill 'discovery-integration-worker' doesn't exist in .factory/skills/. Worker followed procedure=true but there was no skill file to verify against.

rejectedObservations.observation: services.yaml doesn't document verification commands (npm run test:repo etc.)
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented via npm run scripts; services.yaml documents operational service start/stop/health not repo-level validation commands

rejectedObservations.observation: Credential/readiness validator pattern not codified in library
rejectedObservations.reason: Pattern is well-established in the codebase; codifying it would be adding guidance beyond factual knowledge. Library is for discovered facts, not implementation guidance.

rejectedObservations.observation: Automated checks for DiscoveryRunTracker transitions not added despite feature description mentioning it
rejectedObservations.reason: The feature's primary goal (restore handleDiscoverySetupDeepLink + tests passing) was achieved. The gap is a deferred item, not a failure of the delivered feature. The feature description mentions it but the core verification (tests passing) is satisfied.
